setting.note: A hot July in the Haymarket district, 1865. Dostoevsky censored his own street names, and this walk reconstructs them.

rights.originalWork: Public domain worldwide. Dostoevsky died in 1881, so the work is out of copyright everywhere.
rights.translation: Constance Garnett, 1914. Garnett died in 1946, so the translation is public domain in life-plus-70 territories (since 2017) and in the United States (published before 1929).
rights.textSource: Standard Ebooks (public domain dedication), from a Project Gutenberg transcription
rights.territoryNotes: The work is free everywhere. The Garnett translation is free in the United States and in life-plus-70 territories. A different translation is a different rights object, and every quote here is checked against Garnett's.
rights.attribution: Fyodor Dostoevsky, Crime and Punishment (1866), translated by Constance Garnett (1914).

palette.accentSourceQuote: the little yellow room that was like a cupboard or a box
palette.note: Petersburg yellow. Yellow is the novel's colour of decay and sickness: Raskolnikov's yellow garret, Sonia's yellow ticket, the pawnbroker's yellow room, faces yellow with illness. A bright yellow fails WCAG AA on paper, and the darker, jaundiced ochre that passes is closer to what Dostoevsky means anyway.

sonia.name: Sonia Marmeladov
sonia.colorSourceQuote: he had left her in the middle of the room in her green shawl

svidrigailov.name: Arkady Svidrigailov
svidrigailov.colorSourceQuote: a fog had fallen upon him
